instruction: Create a SwiftUI view for visionOS 26 that uses the new `.volumetricShadow(radius: depth:)` modifier to cast a realistic, soft shadow from a 3D Text element.
Refactor this old ObservableObject class into a modern Swift class using the @Observable macro for OS26.
Write the code for a configurable WidgetKit widget for iOS 26 that allows a user to select a specific SF Symbol to display using the modern App Intents framework.
For macOS 26, create a SwiftUI Table that uses the new `.tableColumnStyle(.insetGrouped)` to display a list of files with their sizes.
Create an interactive complication for watchOS 26 that increments a counter each time it's tapped, using the latest interactive widget APIs.
Generate a SwiftUI view for iOS 26 that utilizes the new `.liveActivityTint(_:)` modifier to dynamically color a Live Activity based on its state.
Write a visionOS 26 view that creates a Portal and displays a 2D settings view inside of it, positioned 2 meters in front of the user.
Refactor this ObservableObject that manages a collection to use the modern @Observable macro and a simple array.
For iPadOS 26, create a view that uses the new `.adaptivePopover(sourceRect:in:)` modifier to present a popover from a precise location on the screen.
Create a simple macOS 26 MenuBarExtra that uses a SwiftData query to display the number of items in a database.
Write a TVOS 26 view that uses the `.focusEffect(.parallax)` modifier to create a deep parallax effect on a card view when it's focused.
Refactor this simple counter class from ObservableObject to use the @Observable macro.
Create a WidgetKit widget for iOS 26 that displays data fetched from a URL and uses the new `.widgetRefreshPolicy(.after(date))` modifier to schedule updates.
For visionOS 26, write a view that toggles between a fully immersive space and a mixed-immersion space using the latest scene presentation APIs.
Create a SwiftUI view for iOS 26 that uses the new `.shareSheet(items:preview:)` modifier to provide a custom preview when sharing content.
Refactor a SwiftUI view's state management from using a simple @StateObject to the modern @State and @Observable pattern.
For watchOS 26, create a complication that shows a circular gauge and updates its value based on an App Intent.
Write a macOS 26 view that uses the `.fileImporter` modifier to let a user select a text file and display its contents.
Create a WidgetKit App Intent for OS26 that takes a user-provided string and updates the widget's content.
For visionOS 26, design a view that presents an ornamental 3D model using the new `.ornament(attachmentAnchor:contentAlignment:)` modifier.
Create a Lock Screen widget for iOS 26 that displays the current weather temperature.
Refactor this class that uses a Combine publisher to announce changes to use the @Observable macro instead.
Write a visionOS 26 view that reacts to a pinch gesture to scale a Model3D view up and down.
Create a simple macOS 26 app that uses a Settings scene to manage user preferences.
For iOS 26, create a view that uses the new `.sensor(type:action:)` modifier to react to accelerometer data.
Write a WidgetKit provider for OS26 that uses an App Intent to find a specific item to display.
Refactor this view model with multiple @Published properties to a single @Observable class.
For tvOS 26, create a view that displays a horizontally scrolling list of cards, optimized for focus navigation.
Create a visionOS 26 view that uses a hover effect to highlight an interactive element.
Write an iOS 26 Live Activity that displays a timer counting down.
Refactor this SwiftUI view that manually passes an ObservableObject through the environment to one that uses the modern @Environment and @Observable pattern.
For watchOS 26, create a view that uses the digital crown to scroll through a list of items.
Create a WidgetKit App Intent for macOS 26 that lets the user pick a folder whose contents will be displayed in the widget.
Write a SwiftUI view for iOS 26 that uses the new `.inspector(isPresented:)` modifier to show a detail inspector pane.
For visionOS 26, write a view that tracks a user's hand gestures to control the position of a sphere in 3D space.
Refactor a complex data model from a class hierarchy of ObservableObjects to a simpler structure using the @Observable macro.
Create a SwiftUI view for iOS 26 that uses the updated PhotosPicker to select multiple images and display them in a grid.
For macOS 26, create a document-based app using SwiftData for persistence.
Write a WidgetKit widget for watchOS 26 that uses `push-to-talk` to allow quick audio messaging.
For tvOS 26, create a view that uses the `.rotaryFocus(direction:action:)` modifier to handle input from a rotary remote.
Refactor this code that uses NotificationCenter to update state into a modern approach using the @Observable macro and direct property observation.
Create a visionOS 26 view that uses a particle emitter to create a celebratory confetti effect.
Write an iOS 26 view that uses the new `.safeAreaTransition(.animated)` modifier for smoother Safe Area animations.
Create a WidgetKit widget for macOS 26 that is resizable and adapts its content based on its size.
For watchOS 26, write a view that uses the HealthKit API to display the user's current heart rate.
Refactor this code to use the modern @Observable macro, ensuring that nested observable objects also trigger view updates.
Create a tvOS 26 app that uses the new `.ambientVideoPlayer` modifier to play a subtle background video.
Write a visionOS 26 view that detects when a user looks at a specific object and highlights it.
Create a simple iOS 26 app that uses SwiftData to store and display a list of to-do items.
